Rafael Mendes: pulling a thread from the last couple incidents + workshop parking lot: edge cases where it’s clearly multi-cause (capacity + routing, capacity + auth, etc). we need a simple rule for “primary incident_type” vs “contributing factors” so reporting isn’t garbage.

text:
Rafael Mendes: pulling a thread from the last couple incidents + workshop parking lot: edge cases where it’s clearly multi-cause (capacity + routing, capacity + auth, etc). we need a simple rule for “primary incident_type” vs “contributing factors” so reporting isn’t garbage.

Rafael Mendes: prompt: incident commander is in the channel, sees 5xx, sees router falling back a ton, and fleet is paging “regional GPUs saturated”. what do we want as primary type? :thinking_face:

Mina Haddad: +1 we keep arguing this in real time. my take: if routing is behaving as designed (fallbacks happening because upstream capacity), primary should be capacity/fleet. if routing is misbehaving (wrong region/model selected, stuck to unhealthy pool, fallback loop), primary should be routing.

Vanessa Ortiz: agree in spirit. problem is “routing behaving as designed” is hard to know early. on-call sees fallba...

Irene Choi: ack. we can support pool-level toggles in fleet manager but need the exact mechanism/keys. Benji/Ethan can you confirm what the runtime reads?

text:
Rafael Mendes: post-launch metrics check for hot-route protection (GA enablement window ended ~2h ago). Can folks sanity check: tail latency + 5xx for protected routes vs last week? any weirdness w/ shed/429 reason codes?

Sergio Costa: looking at the "Hot Route Protection - Enterprise" dashboard (us-east + eu-west). Early read is good.
- /v1/chat/completions enterprise p99: 3.9s -> 2.6s (24h rolling)
- /v1/embeddings enterprise p99: 1.3s -> 0.9s
- 5xx rate: down ~45% on protected routes (mostly timeouts)
Annotations line up w/ flag enablement.

Aisha Bello: +1. Error budget burn improved too.
- Chat enterprise SLO (99.9%): burn rate was ~2.1x during peaks last week; now hovering ~0.7x since rollout.
- Best-effort getting throttled earlier (expected), but seeing fewer fleet-wide latency spikes.
I’m not seeing any paging storms from the new alerts either (nice).

title: #eng-sre: 1763512044
preview:
Sean Gallagher: seeing a bunch of variance in the baseline sweeps this week (esp p99 TTFT). before we chase runtime ghosts, can we align on “benchmark fleet hygiene” + a standard warmup/isolation protocol?

Rina Kobayashi: +1. same commit / same manifest, but L40S runs in us-east swing ~8-12% tokens/s. feels like pl...
text:
Sean Gallagher: seeing a bunch of variance in the baseline sweeps this week (esp p99 TTFT). before we chase runtime ghosts, can we align on “benchmark fleet hygiene” + a standard warmup/isolation protocol?

Rina Kobayashi: +1. same commit / same manifest, but L40S runs in us-east swing ~8-12% tokens/s. feels like placement/noisy neighbor.

Claire Dubois: methodology-wise we need to be able to say “this delta is real” vs “this was a different box having a bad day”. what are the top suspected sources?

Allison Grant: in order of likelihood from past infra perf stuff:
- GPU clocks not pinned / auto-boost variance
- power limits inconsistent across hosts
- thermal throttling (esp if other jobs just ran)
- NUMA / CPU contention (shared host, bad pinning)
- storage/network noise if artifacts/logging is heavy mid-run
- kernel cache/warm cache effects (first run after deploy)

Aisha Bello: al...

text:
Rafael Mendes: Heads up: starting **next week (Mon)**, `incident_type` will be **required** in incident-bot for all *new* incidents.

Rafael Mendes: TL;DR
- When you create an incident, the Slack modal will require **incident_type** (+ subtype where applicable)
- incident-bot will auto-resolve **primary owner team** from the mapping table (you can override if it’s wrong)
- You won’t be able to mark an incident **Resolved/Closed** without a type + owner (Sev0/Sev1 also needs postmortem link or placeholder)

Rafael Mendes: Why: we can’t keep doing “freeform tags” and then arguing after the fact. This is part of the Reliability Program push for consistent classification + ownership.

Rafael Mendes: Quick refs:
- v1 taxonomy defs + decision tree (WIP but usable): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SRE/Incident+taxonomy+v1
- owner mapping + remediation SLAs: https://confluence...

title: #eng-sre: 1762730229
preview:

Rafael Mendes: yeah, on-call got pinged twice this morning. we need to...
text:
Aisha Bello: heads up: since we flipped canary protection in us-east, we’re getting a lot of near-pages from the “overload shedding” alerts. This is expected best-effort 429s doing their job, but the alerts are basically instantaneous and noisy.

Rafael Mendes: yeah, on-call got pinged twice this morning. we need to make sure “best-effort getting shed” != page. the whole point is to keep enterprise stable.

Allison Grant: agree. we should separate:
1) notification/annotation when protection activates (for awareness)
2) paging only when enterprise SLO is at risk OR protection is behaving unexpectedly (e.g., enterprise being shed, or 5xx rises)

Sergio Costa: the current rule is basically `shed_rate_total > X for 5m` and it doesn’t filter tier. so any burst that triggers best-effort admission control flaps it.

Logan Wright: +1. we’re emitting `ri_overload_shed_requests_total{tier=...,r...

title: #eng-sre: 1762140092
preview:
Allison Grant: heads up on the incident taxonomy work — adding `incident_type` as a label on request/route metrics is going to be a cardinality footgun if we’re not super strict.

Allison Grant: even if we say “top-level only”, in practice folks will want subtype, team, model, region, route, etc. and then Mimir/Prom...
text:
Allison Grant: heads up on the incident taxonomy work — adding `incident_type` as a label on request/route metrics is going to be a cardinality footgun if we’re not super strict.

Allison Grant: even if we say “top-level only”, in practice folks will want subtype, team, model, region, route, etc. and then Mimir/Prom storage + query perf gets spicy.

Rafael Mendes: +1, this came up in the kickoff. can you spell out the constraints we should bake into ENG-4826 so we don’t end up arguing per-PR?

Allison Grant: yep. a few concrete points:
- no freeform labels. no “stringly typed” `incident_type="${whatever}"` from the app.
- allowlist (enum) for `incident_type` values, owned centrally.
- `subtype` should NOT be a metric label on high-volume series. if we need it, put it in logs/span attributes.
- also avoid labeling *request-level* counters/histograms with incident_type. that implies eve...

title: #eng-sre: 1762058123
preview:

text:
Rafael Mendes: Kicking off incident learnings review from the 11/03 Sev1 (fleetwide latency cascade). We need to decide which routes qualify as “protected” in MVP so we can scope rate limits / priority routing. Can we align today?

incident-bot: :spiral_note_pad: Reference: 2025-11-03 Sev1 “fleetwide latency cascade from hot route” (postmortem draft linked in #incidents). Key symptom: p99 chat latency + elevated 5xx across shared pools.

Aisha Bello: +1. MVP needs to be small and very explicit. In the incident we basically got taken out by one or two hot paths + bursty tenants. If we pick too many routes, we’ll drown in config/alert tuning.

Sean Gallagher: Agree. Let’s anchor it to “routes that can degrade the whole fleet” + “routes with contractual enterprise SLOs”. That’s the safety case.

Allison Grant: From observability side, we also want to avoid high-cardinality per-model/per-...

title: #eng-sre: 1762035580
preview:
Rafael Mendes: Need to land remediation SLAs today-ish for the taxonomy doc. Main open q: what’s *realistic* for Sev1 in Dedicated (TTM + TTF). Also: do we carve out cloud-provider outages / upstream capacity events? Looking for opinions + data.

Lauren Bishop: Dedicated customers are going to hold us to whatever we...
text:
Rafael Mendes: Need to land remediation SLAs today-ish for the taxonomy doc. Main open q: what’s *realistic* for Sev1 in Dedicated (TTM + TTF). Also: do we carve out cloud-provider outages / upstream capacity events? Looking for opinions + data.

Lauren Bishop: Dedicated customers are going to hold us to whatever we publish internally, even if not 1:1 in contract. If we say “30m mitigate” for Sev1 Dedicated we will miss it a lot.

Vanessa Ortiz: +1. Sev1 Dedicated is often capacity/fleet in a specific region or cluster. Sometimes mitigation is “move traffic / add burst / swap pool” which can be 45-90m depending on GPU availability.

Harish Reddy: For Dedicated, mitigation frequently requires provisioning (or rebalancing) which is not instant. If we set <60m, we’ll create churn and “SLA breach alert fatigue.”

Aisha Bello: Can we define Sev1 Dedicated TTM as 60m *for customer-impacting...

Rafael Mendes: circling back on statuspage v2: we need to settle anti-flap + thresholds for when we mark a component Degraded vs Partial Outage

Allison Grant: +1. we keep getting toggles on transient p95 spikes and it makes support’s life worse

Christine Wong: the PRD wants “consistent + actionable” not “every blip”. so I’m leaning: status should change only when it’s customer-noticeable AND sustained

Miles Thornton: reminder: statuspage is *customer comms*, not our alerting system. anti-flap should bias conservative and require human confirmation for sev0/1 anyway

Rafael Mendes: ok proposals on the table:
1) hysteresis thresholds (enter/exit different)
2) min duration before publish
3) cool-down before auto-clearing

title: #eng-sre: 1759882210
preview:
Rafael Mendes: Kicking off a thread on status page component taxonomy. We need to stop calling everything “API” :grimacing:. Proposal: split Hosted API vs Streaming vs Embeddings (and maybe Reranking) + split by region. Want feedback on shape + naming.

Christine Wong: +1. Current single “API” component is killing u...
text:
Rafael Mendes: Kicking off a thread on status page component taxonomy. We need to stop calling everything “API” :grimacing:. Proposal: split Hosted API vs Streaming vs Embeddings (and maybe Reranking) + split by region. Want feedback on shape + naming.

Christine Wong: +1. Current single “API” component is killing us in comms. Customers see “Operational” while embeddings is on fire.

Mina Haddad: For region split, do we want cloud region names (us-east-1/us-west-2/eu-west-1) or friendly (US East/US West/EU West)? Friendly is better for customers, but mapping has to be unambiguous.

Allison Grant: Observability side: we can tag metrics by `region` already; the gateway has `pop` and `cluster` labels. Friendly display name is fine as long as component_id uses canonical region key.

Aisha Bello: If we do product x region, number of components blows up quickly. What’s our target component ...

title: #eng-sre: 1749573308
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Post-incident quick notes while it’s fresh (benchmark landing page latency / intermittent 5xx during launch traffic).

Sean Gallagher: TL;DR root cause: CDN cache config + cache key mismatch led to a cache-miss storm after deploy. We expected the benchmark JSON payload to be edge-cached, but requests...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Post-incident quick notes while it’s fresh (benchmark landing page latency / intermittent 5xx during launch traffic).

Sean Gallagher: TL;DR root cause: CDN cache config + cache key mismatch led to a cache-miss storm after deploy. We expected the benchmark JSON payload to be edge-cached, but requests were effectively going to origin for most page loads.

Incident Bot: Incident INC-1187 status changed: MONITORING -> RESOLVED (owner: @sean.g)

Neha Kapoor: +1. Specifically: CDN rule for `/benchmarks/results.json` wasn’t honoring `stale-while-revalidate` because origin was returning `Cache-Control: private, max-age=0` for a subset of responses (when auth header present). Those ended up uncacheable at the edge.

Logan Wright: The auth header was coming from the marketing site fetch wrapper. Even though results endpoint is “public”, the wrapper was attaching `Authorization:...

text:
Sean Gallagher: Heads up — we need launch-week dashboards + alerts for the benchmark landing page + interactive table before EOD tomorrow. Focus: page latency, 5xx, CDN error rates. Also any cache/origin weirdness since we’re serving results JSON.

Sean Gallagher: @Neha Kapoor can you sanity-check alert thresholds / oncall coverage? @Felix Schneider want to confirm the key endpoints + what “good” looks like for the UX.

Neha Kapoor: yep. Let’s keep it tight + actionable. Primary is end-user experience: page load + table data fetch. Secondary: origin health + CDN.

Felix Schneider: endpoints:
- marketing site page: `/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar` (plus `/benchmarks` redirect)
- results fetch from site hits `GET /api/benchmarks/v1/public/latest.json` (served by the benchmark results service + cached)
- CSV export: `GET /api/benchmarks/v1/public/latest.csv`

Felix Schneider: UI is prett...

title: #eng-sre: 1748704420
preview:

Rafael Mendes: draft alert rules (names tbd):
1) WARN: max_bound_hit_rate >...
text:
Rafael Mendes: putting up for review: proposed paging thresholds for max-bound saturation + queue buildup for Dedicated autoscaling bounds. goal is catch "we're pinned at max and customers are queuing" without paging on normal bursty stuff.

Rafael Mendes: draft alert rules (names tbd):
1) WARN: max_bound_hit_rate > 0.10 for 15m
2) PAGE: max_bound_hit_rate > 0.25 for 10m AND p95_request_latency_ms > SLO_p95 for 10m
3) PAGE: max_bound_hit_rate > 0.25 for 10m AND queue_depth_p95 > 0 for 10m (if latency SLO not wired for that deployment)

Rafael Mendes: scope: per deployment_id + region. routed to Dedicated oncall (platform-infra) during pilot, then SRE primary after GA.

Aisha Bello: +1 on needing the "AND". if we page on bound_hit alone we'll get hammered any time someone sets max low intentionally.

Miles Thornton: agree. also, bound_hit_rate is new — do we have confidence it isn't go...

title: #eng-sre: 1747805432
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — saw three p95 GPU-util spikes in us-west this morning that paged oncall. All recovered within 30–90s. We should adjust paging vs ticketing to avoid wakeups for these micro-flares. Thoughts?

Ravi Patel: +1. Do we know cardinality? per-pod or cluster-wide?

Maya Ortiz: Cluster-level spikes, not s...
text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — saw three p95 GPU-util spikes in us-west this morning that paged oncall. All recovered within 30–90s. We should adjust paging vs ticketing to avoid wakeups for these micro-flares. Thoughts?

Ravi Patel: +1. Do we know cardinality? per-pod or cluster-wide?

Maya Ortiz: Cluster-level spikes, not single pod. Prometheus shows short-lived 2–3x p95 bumps correlated with scheduling churn.

Luca Marino: Agree noise. Proposal: keep page for node OOM / node offline / sustained >10m above 90%. For p95 spikes that are <5m and not accompanied by OOM/evict — convert to ticket. We can implement with Alertmanager piping to ops-tickets if rule has 'severity: low' and 'duration: <5m'. :white_check_mark:

Ava Chen: Sketch of rule — use promql:
```promql
max_over_time(node_gpu_utilization[5m]) > 0.9
```
But only page if `increase(node_oom_total[15m]) > 0` or `node_up==0` else ticket....

text:
Rafael Mendes: hey all — prepping ENG-4829 game day. need ideas for KMS outage scenarios + who’s doing what (facilitator/observers/comms).

Sean Gallagher: +1. can we cover both “slow + throttled” and “hard down”? last incident pattern was latency spike → retry amplification.

Allison Grant: scenarios I’d like:
1) p95 latency jump (ex: 200ms → 3s) for decrypt/envelope
2) intermittent throttling (429) at ~5-10% then ramp to 50%
3) full outage (timeouts)
4) AccessDenied due to IAM regression
Also want to validate alerts aren’t paging nonstop in staging :)

Tomáš Novák: add “wrong key id / key disabled” too. we’ve seen misconfig look like InvalidCiphertext / NotFound depending on provider.

Rafael Mendes: yep. let’s list them cleanly:
A) Latency degradation
B) Throttling
C) Full outage/timeouts
D) Permission denied
E) Key disabled / scheduled deletion / wrong ARN
F) Ciphertext/key mismat...

text:
Rafael Mendes: Heads up: staging private cluster is paging *a lot* since we turned on the min-pack rules. Can we do a quick noise review today + decide what’s disabled by default for v1?

Rafael Mendes: Top offenders from last 12h:
- `RedwoodHighLatencyP99` (fires during warm-up + batch size changes)
- `RedwoodGPUUtilizationHigh` (basically constant)
- `KubePodCrashLooping` for log-collector when storage is tight
- `PrometheusTargetMissing` (grafana sidecar / dashboards configmap reload)

Neha Kapoor: +1. I pulled Alertmanager history too. A bunch are “technically true” but not actionable/page-worthy for Private customers.

Sean Gallagher: Can you paste the current thresholds / for: durations? Especially for the latency + GPU ones.

Rafael Mendes: Yep. Current rule snippets (from the staging branch):
```
- alert: RedwoodHighLatencyP99
 expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(redwood_r...

title: #eng-sre: 1746297701
preview:
Neha Kapoor: Kicking off severity mapping for Private minimum pack alerts. We need a crisp “page vs ticket” policy so we don’t ship a pager DOS to customers + to us. Can we align on definitions today?

Aisha Bello: +1. Also want this to line up with SLO burn alerts we’re adding. If we say “Sev2 pages” but it’s just ...
text:
Neha Kapoor: Kicking off severity mapping for Private minimum pack alerts. We need a crisp “page vs ticket” policy so we don’t ship a pager DOS to customers + to us. Can we align on definitions today?

Aisha Bello: +1. Also want this to line up with SLO burn alerts we’re adding. If we say “Sev2 pages” but it’s just a warning, we’ll regret it.

Allison Grant: Reminder from standards: severity taxonomy is Sev1/Sev2/Sev3 (page, page, ticket) but teams have been inconsistent. We should standardize for Private pack specifically.

Tomáš Novák: From oncall POV: page only for conditions that are actionable immediately and likely customer-impacting. Everything else should create a ticket (and ideally have runbook links in annotations).

Sean Gallagher: + consider that Private clusters are customer-operated. Our oncall can’t fix their NetworkPolicy at 3am. So “page” should be reserved for (a) d...

title: #eng-sre: 1741586112
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Heads up: we’re going to start doing parallel sales demos off the same “demo tenant” next week and I’m worried we’re under-sizing capacity + quotas. Last 2 dry runs were fine with 1 demo, but 2 in flight could easily trigger 429s / queue spikes.

Markus Klein: yep. had a call yesterday where we ran t...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Heads up: we’re going to start doing parallel sales demos off the same “demo tenant” next week and I’m worried we’re under-sizing capacity + quotas. Last 2 dry runs were fine with 1 demo, but 2 in flight could easily trigger 429s / queue spikes.

Markus Klein: yep. had a call yesterday where we ran the live scenario + synthetic traffic at the same time and it got close. biggest fear is a mid-demo stall.

Allison Grant: What are the current assumptions? How many concurrent demos do we want to support as “normal” vs burst?

Sean Gallagher: Proposal from SRE side:
- Treat demo env like prod-lite: dedicated GPU pool (noisy neighbor protection)
- Hard caps via rate limits (token/sec + concurrent reqs) per demo project
- Synthetic traffic generator must run in a separate project/route w/ its own quota so it can’t starve the live demo

Devon Price: +1 on separating synthetic ...

title: #eng-sre: 1741495210
preview:
Sean Gallagher: proposing we lock demo-env SLOs + error budget policy this week so we have something concrete for alerting + escalation. this is for the *demo tenant* only (not prod). looking for owner confirms + where pages go.

Sean Gallagher: draft SLOs (feel free to bikeshed numbers, but we need something):
1) D...
text:
Sean Gallagher: proposing we lock demo-env SLOs + error budget policy this week so we have something concrete for alerting + escalation. this is for the *demo tenant* only (not prod). looking for owner confirms + where pages go.

Sean Gallagher: draft SLOs (feel free to bikeshed numbers, but we need something):
1) Demo API availability: 99.7% weekly (request success excluding customer-induced 4xx)
2) Demo API latency: p95 < 1.8s TTFT for streaming, p95 < 6.5s for 256 output tokens (measured on seeded synthetic workload)
3) Model pin integrity: 99.9% of requests routed to pinned version (fallback allowed only to approved variant list)
4) Console dashboard freshness: seeded project has non-empty graphs for last 15m window 99% of the time during business hours (Mon-Fri)
5) Reset + healthcheck: `demo reset` completes < 8 min p95; healthcheck green >= 99% of runs

Sean Gallagher: error bud...

title: #eng-sre: 1741124411
preview:
Sean Gallagher: For LTS pilot + GA we need *separate* dashboards/alerts for LTS traffic and patch rollouts. Right now everything rolls up under runtime overall, which makes it hard to tell if 1.18-lts is healthy vs mainline. Can we scope what we want in SLO toolkit / dd dashboards this week?

Sergio Costa: Yep. I’ve...
text:
Sean Gallagher: For LTS pilot + GA we need *separate* dashboards/alerts for LTS traffic and patch rollouts. Right now everything rolls up under runtime overall, which makes it hard to tell if 1.18-lts is healthy vs mainline. Can we scope what we want in SLO toolkit / dd dashboards this week?

Sergio Costa: Yep. I’ve been thinking we need 3 layers:
1) LTS fleet health (by channel=lts)
2) Patch rollout monitoring (by runtime_version=1.18.1-lts, region, cluster)
3) Customer-impact view (Dedicated/Private tenants on LTS)

Allison Grant: +1. Biggest gap: latency SLO views split by version. We’ve had cases where global p95 was fine but the new patch had regressions on A100 only.

Connor O'Brien: Release Eng POV: we need something we can use during patch week. Ideally a “release cockpit” dashboard that shows error budget burn + p50/p95 + throughput per GPU SKU *for the exact tag* we’re rolli...

title: #eng-sre: 1741023321
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock perf regression thresholds for Runtime LTS gates this week. Current proposal in doc is a bit hand-wavy. Can we align on p50/p95 + throughput + what to do when benches are noisy?

Allison Grant: +1. For LTS we should bias towards catching p95 regressions even if it means occasional rer...
text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock perf regression thresholds for Runtime LTS gates this week. Current proposal in doc is a bit hand-wavy. Can we align on p50/p95 + throughput + what to do when benches are noisy?

Allison Grant: +1. For LTS we should bias towards catching p95 regressions even if it means occasional reruns.

Connor O'Brien: Release Eng perspective: thresholds need to be deterministic enough that patch week isn’t "rerun until green". Also need an explicit waiver path (who can override, what evidence required).

Marcus Reed: Here’s what we’ve been using informally for mainline perf-canary:
- latency p50 regression <= +5%
- latency p95 regression <= +8%
- tok/s throughput regression <= -3%
…but the variance on A100 runners is sometimes +/-4% on p95, depending on contention.

Jonas Weber: For LTS, I’d tighten p95, but only if we also change methodology (more repetitions / bet...

text:
Allison Grant: Dropping the cache telemetry incident runbook here for final review before we call it “oncall-ready”: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PLAT/cache-telemetry-pipeline-incident-runbook

Allison Grant: Would love eyes on (1) symptoms/triage flow, (2) ownership + escalation, (3) what we consider a “real cache regression” vs telemetry missing.

Sean Gallagher: Skimmed. This is solid. Biggest ask: make the first 5 mins super explicit. Like “is data missing?” check before we chase cache runtime issues.

Aisha Bello: +1. Also can we add a quick note about expected diurnal dips / workload mix changes so we don’t page ourselves into oblivion.

Sergio Costa: I’ll add a top section: “Sanity checks (no paging)” w/ links to the ingestion drop panel + metric volume panels.

Logan Wright: On telemetry side, the failure modes list is accurate. One tweak: call out label-dro...

title: #eng-sre: 1740816603
preview:
Aisha Bello: hey folks — for the quantization profile beta canary, we need a few new dashboard panels ASAP. can someone confirm metric names + labels so we don’t invent new ones?

Aisha Bello: panels requested (per route + rollup):
- enabled routes (count + % of traffic)
- fallback rate (applied vs fallback vs disab...
text:
Aisha Bello: hey folks — for the quantization profile beta canary, we need a few new dashboard panels ASAP. can someone confirm metric names + labels so we don’t invent new ones?

Aisha Bello: panels requested (per route + rollup):
- enabled routes (count + % of traffic)
- fallback rate (applied vs fallback vs disabled)
- error rate (5xx + policy/runtime errors)
- latency (p50/p95 decode + total)
- cost/token delta if we have it (nice to have)

Aisha Bello: also would like filters: `route_id`, `tenant_id` (or org), `model_id`, `region`, `deployment_tier`.

Logan Wright: :eyes: yep, we should align on names. what’s currently exported from runtime vs control plane? (don’t want 2 sources).

Caleb Johnson: runtime already emits request-level counters + histos w/ `quant_profile_id` annotation (post-18555). control plane has config state but not request truth.

Nadia Rahman: cost/token delt...

title: #eng-sre: 1740786631
preview:
Sean Gallagher: seeing a dip on the cache hit-rate dashboard in hosted. question for oncall policy: do we page on this kind of hit-rate drop or just ticket it?

Sean Gallagher: screenshot-ish numbers (global hosted): prefix_hr down ~0.44 -> ~0.36 over ~25m. kv reuse also down a bit. no customer-reported issues yet.
...
text:
Sean Gallagher: seeing a dip on the cache hit-rate dashboard in hosted. question for oncall policy: do we page on this kind of hit-rate drop or just ticket it?

Sean Gallagher: screenshot-ish numbers (global hosted): prefix_hr down ~0.44 -> ~0.36 over ~25m. kv reuse also down a bit. no customer-reported issues yet.

Allison Grant: pls don’t page on hit-rate drop alone. we said guardrail only unless there’s evidence of regression (pressure/evictions/telemetry missing). does it line up w/ memory pressure?

Sean Gallagher: checking now. pressure gauge looks flat-ish, hovering ~0.62. evictions not spiking. wasted prefill ratio up though (0.08 -> 0.12).

Aisha Bello: wasted prefill can go up with traffic mix shifts too (more novel prompts / longer first turns). did prompt length buckets shift?

Sean Gallagher: yeah, prompt-length histogram shifted toward 4k-8k bucket in the last hour. also...

title: #eng-sre: 1740775807
preview:
Sergio Costa: Heads up: after the last couple burst beta exercises, the new burst alerts are *chatty*. We had ~14 pages in 24h, most looked like short-lived saturation / scrape gaps. Want to tune before we re-enable for the next beta tenants.

Sean Gallagher: yeah agree. goal is still: fast detection for neighbor im...
text:
Sergio Costa: Heads up: after the last couple burst beta exercises, the new burst alerts are *chatty*. We had ~14 pages in 24h, most looked like short-lived saturation / scrape gaps. Want to tune before we re-enable for the next beta tenants.

Sean Gallagher: yeah agree. goal is still: fast detection for neighbor impact + protect non-burst SLOs. But we can’t page on every 90s blip.

Allison Grant: I pulled the alert history. Biggest offenders:
- `BurstPoolSaturationHigh` firing on single-scrape spikes (pool_free_gpus dips to 0 for 1-2 samples)
- `BurstDenialRateHigh` in eu-west during telemetry lag (denial reasons flip to `TELEMETRY_STALE` for ~3-4m)
- label cardinality causing Grafana panel timeouts (per-tenant + per-model labels on the same alert)

Rafael Mendes: +1 on the cardinality. Also the page noise made oncall start ignoring the channel which is… not ideal.

Peter Holtz: from...

title: #eng-sre: 1740662244
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock observability requirements for retention purge jobs before ENG starts wiring more stores. What are the *must-have* metrics + alerts + runbook content? Also please call out label/cardinality landmines.

Allison Grant: +1. If we ship this without sane cardinality we’re gonna regret it. ...
text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to lock observability requirements for retention purge jobs before ENG starts wiring more stores. What are the *must-have* metrics + alerts + runbook content? Also please call out label/cardinality landmines.

Allison Grant: +1. If we ship this without sane cardinality we’re gonna regret it. Let’s define:
- metrics names + labels
- dashboard panels
- alert thresholds (page vs ticket)
- runbook: how to re-run + how to confirm deletes + how to throttle

Logan Wright: From telemetry side, we can emit from purge job framework + per-store adapters. Current draft metric list:
- `retention_ttl_lag_seconds` (gauge)
- `retention_purge_rows_deleted_total` (counter)
- `retention_purge_failures_total` (counter)
- `retention_purge_backlog_rows` (gauge) OR backlog by oldest row age
- `retention_purge_job_duration_seconds` (histogram)
- `retention_purge_job_runs_total` (count...

title: #eng-sre: 1740499033
preview:
Allison Grant: seeing a bunch of questions in the last few days: “hit-rate dipped” vs “metrics stopped flowing”. we need a clear way to detect ingestion drops for the new cache telemetry before we start paging ppl on fake regressions

Aisha Bello: +1. right now dashboards show a clean cliff to 0 and everyone assumes...
text:
Allison Grant: seeing a bunch of questions in the last few days: “hit-rate dipped” vs “metrics stopped flowing”. we need a clear way to detect ingestion drops for the new cache telemetry before we start paging ppl on fake regressions

Aisha Bello: +1. right now dashboards show a clean cliff to 0 and everyone assumes regression. but for cache, 0 can mean either “no hits” or “we’re missing the signal”

Sean Gallagher: the worst case is we page oncall for “cache efficiency SLO burn” and it’s just pipeline / label-drop

Logan Wright: from telemetry side we have a couple failure modes:
- prometheus remote_write backlog / drop
- ingestion cardinality guard drops (label not allowed -> series dropped)
- exporter disabled via flag (env config)
- runtime emitting but gateway tags missing -> metrics arrive but unsliceable (looks like “all unknown”)

Nadia Rahman: we should explicitly model a “da...
